revised: 2026-07-16 (third pass: every production_only_no_repository_source entry re-searched across all 50 origin/* branches, by filename and by content, before being re-classified; see per-entry verification_note fields)
method: Exact filename-prefix match against the live production ledger (list_migrations) first; if absent, match by ledger name against repository filename suffix or substring; classification distinguishes legacy short-form (8-digit authoring-date) filenames -- expected to differ from the applied timestamp by established convention -- from a non-matching full 14-digit precise-timestamp prefix, which is genuine unreconciled drift.

classification_scheme.note: Third pass replaces the original 4-value enum (exact_match, legacy_pre_tracking, wrong_prefix_unreconciled, production_only_no_repository_source) with this 6-value one. exact_match -> exact_on_main and legacy_pre_tracking -> legacy_pre_tracking_on_main are pure renames (same 33 and 68 entries respectively, no reclassification, no content change beyond the label). production_only_no_repository_source is retired and its 12 entries individually re-searched and re-classified into the four newer buckets below; see each entry's verification_note.
classification_scheme.exact_on_main: Filename-prefix (timestamp) and content match a file currently on origin/main.
classification_scheme.legacy_pre_tracking_on_main: An older/differently-structured migration on main that predates this project's migration-hygiene tracking effort but is clearly the same logical change.
classification_scheme.source_on_non_main_branch_or_history: The source file was found via git history/branch search (not on current main) -- the exact branch and file path are cited in repository_candidate and/or verification_note.
classification_scheme.content_equivalent_source: No filename match anywhere, but a file (on main or another branch) contains equivalent DDL/content that accomplishes the same schema change under a different name -- the file and the equivalence are cited in verification_note.
classification_scheme.production_only_no_source_found: Exhaustively searched (all remote branches, by filename and by content, plus application-code cross-references) and genuinely found nothing. Narrower and more honest than the retired label production_only_no_repository_source, which only meant 'not on main', not 'searched everywhere and found nothing'.
classification_scheme.wrong_prefix_unreconciled: A file exists (main or another branch) that is clearly the same migration by content, but its filename's timestamp prefix does not match the production ledger's recorded version -- this repo's recurring reconciliation-class bug.

entries.verification_note: supabase/migrations/20260506_pg_cron_pg_net_setup.sql (main; one of the 52 pre-cutover-legacy files applied to the now-inactive us-east-2 project qpzopweonveumvuqkqgw) contains 'create extension if not exists pg_cron' and 'create extension if not exists pg_net' -- the only CREATE EXTENSION statements found anywhere in supabase/migrations/ on any branch searched. 20260518193933 is the first migration ever applied to the current ca-central-1 project (created 2026-05-18, per list_projects), so it necessarily re-enables whatever extensions the app depends on for a fresh project; pg_cron and pg_net are the only ones this repository's history shows being deliberately enabled anywhere. This is content equivalence for the extensions this file's name plausibly covers, not a byte-identical recovery -- the production migration may also enable other extensions (e.g. pgcrypto) not otherwise visible in this repository's migration history.

Searched all 50 remote branches by filename and by content (git grep for format_taxonomy across every branch's supabase/migrations/*.sql and application code) -- no match anywhere. docs/CONTENT_STUDIO_SEO_AEO_SPEC.md:20 lists this exact filename as a source artifact read on 2026-07-02, and the companion supabase/TASK_12_RECONCILIATION_PLAN.md and RUNBOOK_20260626_content_studio_apply.md files it cites alongside it also do not exist on any branch found -- corroborating the migration was real and applied, but its SQL was never committed anywhere this search reached.

Searched all 50 remote branches by filename and by content (git grep for doctrine_p0) -- no match anywhere. Same corroborating-but-unrecoverable situation as 20260626100000 and 20260626100200: listed in docs/CONTENT_STUDIO_SEO_AEO_SPEC.md:21 as a source artifact read on 2026-07-02, but the file itself was never committed to any branch found.

Searched all 50 remote branches by filename and by content -- no DDL file found anywhere. src/app/api/admin/content-studio/pieces/route.ts:72-73 explicitly mirrors 'the content_pieces_format_check CHECK constraint (supabase/migrations/20260626100200_content_studio_compliance_formats.sql)' with a 10-value format allowlist, and docs/CONTENT_STUDIO_SEO_AEO_SPEC.md:22 lists the same filename as a source artifact read on 2026-07-02 -- strong corroboration the migration is real and what it did (expanding the content_pieces.format CHECK constraint), but the migration file itself was never committed to any branch found, so this is corroborating evidence, not a recovered source.

Searched all 50 remote branches by filename and by content for screened_conflict_checks and conflict_check fragments -- no CREATE TABLE or migration file found anywhere (the only conflict_check-named migration in this repository, 20260414_conflict_check.sql, creates a different, older, unrelated conflict_check table that predates this one). src/lib/matter-stage-gate.ts:80-84 and multiple routes/tests (src/app/api/portal/[firmId]/conflict-checks/*, src/lib/__tests__/matter-conflict-gate.test.ts) query screened_conflict_checks extensively and explicitly assert the legacy conflict_checks table is never queried -- the table is unambiguously real and in active use, but its creating migration was never committed to any branch found.

Searched all 50 remote branches by filename and by content for voice_turn_sessions -- no match anywhere, including in application code (unlike screened_conflict_checks, no route or lib file on any branch references a voice_turn_sessions table). No corroborating evidence beyond the ledger entry itself was found.

Searched all 50 remote branches by filename (no 20260712-dated file exists anywhere) and by content (git grep for fee_detail, firm_profile, firm_fee_, profile_fee across every branch's migrations and application code) -- no match. Two other, distinct fee-related migrations exist on main (20260617_firm_onboarding_v2_fields.sql adds fee_structure to intake_firms; 20260625_firm_onboarding_v2_phase1_bing_apple_fees.sql adds fees_upload_storage_path/fees_freetext/fees_structured, itself currently unreconciled against the ledger too) but neither matches this migration's name or an obvious firm_profile object, and no table named firm_profile or firm_profiles exists anywhere in this repository's history. Genuinely not found after exhaustive search.

Not on main; found on origin/feat/deliverable-suggestions-release at supabase/migrations/20260713185808_pdf_artifact_integrity.sql. Content confirmed matching: adds asset_sha256 and asset_validation columns (plus format/object CHECK constraints and an index) to public.deliverable_versions -- exactly the objects this report's own finding 1 confirmed live in production via information_schema/pg_indexes. The branch file's own timestamp prefix (20260713185808) is 41 seconds off the ledger's recorded applied version (20260713185849); once this branch merges, the file will still need a wrong_prefix_unreconciled-style rename unless corrected on merge.

Not on main; found on origin/feat/deliverable-suggestions-release at supabase/migrations/20260713234632_deliverable_suggestions.sql. Creates public.deliverable_suggestions with the exact shape (author_role/operation/annotation/original_text/replacement_text and their CHECK constraints) this report's finding 1 already confirmed is live in production. Branch file timestamp (20260713234632) is about 2m7s off the ledger's recorded version (20260713234759).

Not on main; found on origin/feat/deliverable-suggestions-release at supabase/migrations/20260713235900_deliverable_suggestion_atomic_workflow.sql. Defines create_deliverable_suggestion_atomic() and its paired apply function as SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, matching the ledger name exactly. Branch file timestamp (20260713235900) is about 4m5s later than the ledger's recorded version (20260713235455) -- the unusual direction (branch file postdates the recorded production apply time) is most likely a later re-touch/rebase of the branch's local file; the content match itself is unambiguous given the identical function name and adjacent-migration sequence on the branch.

Not on main; found on origin/feat/deliverable-suggestions-release at supabase/migrations/20260713235930_deliverable_suggestion_fk_indexes.sql. Adds deliverable_suggestions_deliverable_idx and deliverable_suggestion_events_resulting_version_idx, matching the ledger name exactly. Branch file timestamp (20260713235930) is about 16m34s before the ledger's recorded version (20260714001604).

Not on main; found on origin/feat/deliverable-suggestions-release at supabase/migrations/20260714004511_deliverable_suggestion_release_hardening.sql. Matches the ledger name exactly and re-defines create_deliverable_suggestion_atomic() with the five hardening changes described in its own header comment (serializing suggestion creation, rejecting stale/non-reviewable versions, blocking approval during an open suggestion, clearing the review-notification stamp, validating change-request linkage). Branch file timestamp (20260714004511) is about 34m39s before the ledger's recorded version (20260714011950).
